Your diary date (if set) and watched status for this film remain publicly visible. Ratings on Close Friends and You entries don’t contribute to stats. Add members to your Close Friends from their profile. Set your account default for Privacy mode in Settings.

Draft entries don’t appear in your watched films or diary for others, and don’t contribute to your stats.

Pinned reviews

More

Hail, Caesar!

2016
Watched 02 Nov 2022 1

What’s it all mean anyway? Would that it twere so simple.
You think you are getting somewhere as a deconstructed Rome and its monuments appear on a Hollywood sound stage in an America that is blazing bright, coming out of world war 2, donning the mantle of the world’s superpower for the next 50 years. Not without its threats and enemies, a discontented set of communist writers perceive the writing on the wall and seek compensation, or seek to serve…

Translated from by

Tenet

2020
Watched 22 Sep 2020 10

Tenet is the worst Nolan film
and don’t try to convince me that
it all makes sense in the end
Because, when you take a closer look
The film is built on crazy ideas.
Even if
Some scenes really shine
The plot and characters don’t hold together.
And it’s not true that
Nolan knows how to write emotions
Because
The characters can be believable
Only if there was heart in the script
It’s not true that this movie is good…

Translated from by

Recent reviews

Lessons of Darkness

1992
Watched 13 Mar 2026

ty Claude:

How much oil has humanity used?

Commercial oil extraction only began around 165 years ago — so we’re really talking about industrial-era consumption. A total of roughly 1,572 billion barrels of crude oil were produced historically from 1900 through 2024. To put that in perspective, the world currently consumes around 37.4 billion barrels per year, or about 102.5 million barrels per day. 

How much is left?

This depends heavily on how you define “reserves”:
 ∙ Proven reserves (high…

Translated from by

Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker

2019
Rewatched 10 Mar 2026

As an ally of adoption, I support the ending of this movie.

Sheev Palpatine, the greatest project manager of his generation?

Furiosa: A Mad Max Saga

2024
Watched 20 May 2024 5

By far the best 3 star blockbuster movie of the year. I am the biggest Fury Road fan there is. I was the first, and no one knows the depth of my love, ok? Now, lightning striking a bottle full of lightning is difficult to do so it's no surprise that Furiosa doesn't hit quite like it's predecessor.

I can't quite put my finger on it, but there is some odd pacing/rhythm to the whole picture. Even though the movie…

Translated from by

Dune: Part Two

2024
Watched 21 Feb 2024 14

The white savior we have been waiting for.

The entire movie is in the IMAX format so if you don’t see it in IMAX you will be seeing a different movie. James Cameron creates unique theater experiences, and Villenueve and crew have done the same. The marriage of scale, creative vision, and a story the size of the known universe is unlike anything I have seen before. Tears streaming down my face as Paul chooses a name.

Part 1 built…

Translated from by

Letterboxd is an independent service created by a small team, and we rely mostly on the support of our members to maintain our site and apps. Please consider upgrading to a Pro account—for less than a couple bucks a month, you’ll get cool additional features like all-time and annual stats pages (example), the ability to select (and filter by) your favorite streaming services, and no ads!
